content: I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if it's better than Facebook or just another way to get distracted from my life. The features are always changing so it's hard to find what you're looking for and sometimes the ads are really annoying. On the other hand the photography community is amazing and I love discovering new accounts and getting inspiration for my own feed. Overall I'd say Instagram is fine but it's not like it's revolutionized my social media experience or anything.

I've been using Instagram for years and its still my go-to platform for staying connected with friends and family but sometimes I feel like the algorithm is more focused on showing me ads than actual content that interests me which can be frustrating because at the end of the day you want to see what your friends are up to not a bunch of irrelevant sponsored posts.

I've been using Instagram for years and it's still my go-to platform for staying connected with friends and family. The new algorithm changes have definitely made it more challenging to see content from accounts I don't interact with regularly but overall I think the app is still a great way to discover new things and get inspired by creators.

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's still one of my favorite social media platforms I'm starting to feel like it's just becoming too much of a numbers game everyone's trying to get more followers and likes and it's making the app feel less personal.

I was using Instagram for hours and I didn't really notice how much time had passed until my phone died completely no complaints about functionality but the battery life is still a bit wonky sometimes

I've been using Instagram for years and while I still enjoy posting updates about my daily life and keeping up with friends family events it's gotten to the point where the algorithm prioritizes ads over actual content which makes the whole experience feel more like a chore than a fun way to stay connected online

I've been using Instagram for a few years now and I'm neither here nor there about it honestly. The new feature where you can edit previous posts is kinda cool but also kinda frustrating because sometimes it messes up my aesthetic vibe and I have to spend too much time fixing it. On the other hand the algorithm changes are always so confusing and I end up missing out on some great content because I'm not sure what's trending anymore.

I was getting really good at using Instagram to plan my days and connect with friends but then I had some issues with notifications not working properly which made me really frustrated however after they fixed the problem everything is back to normal now.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if I love it or hate it. The app is always updating so there's always something new to discover but sometimes that means losing features I liked from before. Overall I'd say Instagram is a solid social media platform but it's just okay – nothing special about it.

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's definitely made my life more visually interesting I still think it's a bit of a mixed bag sometimes the content is really high quality but other times you just get a bunch of ads thrown at you and not much else that being said I do love seeing what my friends are up to and discovering new accounts from people who have amazing photography skills

I've been using Instagram for a few years now and I'm still not sure if it's worth the hype it gets - sometimes the algorithm is spot on but other times it feels like they're just showing me ads to make up for lack of meaningful content from accounts I actually care about which can be frustrating when all my favorite creators are posting new stuff.

I've been using Instagram for years and while its features have improved a lot I still find myself getting distracted by all the endless scrolling through my feed sometimes feels like more of a chore than enjoying sharing moments with friends
I've been using Instagram for years and overall its a solid app but lately the algorithm changes are making it feel like less of a social platform and more of a curated highlight reel

I've been using Instagram for years and while I don't mind scrolling through my feed sometimes it feels like they're trying to sell me stuff all the time which is annoying but on the other hand the features are pretty great like Reels and IGTV can be really fun when you're feeling creative so overall its a solid app just don't expect too much in terms of actual functionality.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if it's worth my time. The new features are nice but they're not enough to keep me engaged. I mainly use it to catch up with friends who live far away so it's good for that purpose. However the ads are super annoying and sometimes the app just freezes on me which is really frustrating.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I've never had a major issue with it I've started to notice that the algorithm seems to prioritize accounts I don't really care about over my actual favorites.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the creative features like Reels and IGTV my main account has become a bit of an advertising space with too many sponsored posts and not enough organic content anymore feel like they're making money off me rather than showing me what their users are actually sharing
I've been using Instagram for years and I'm not really sure what's changed lately but it feels like they're trying to force everyone into this curated lifestyle that just doesn't work for me anymore.

I've tried using Instagram more often lately and while I see some cool content from my friends and artists I don't feel like I'm really engaging with the platform much anymore because every time I open it I see a bunch of ads which is kind of annoying.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm not sure what's changed but the algorithm seems to be showing me way more ads than ever before while also limiting my visibility to good content from accounts I actually care about it feels like they're trying to make money off of us instead of just giving us a clean feed

I've been using Instagram for years and overall its a solid app but lately I'm not sure if its still worth my time. The new features are nice but sometimes they feel like just cluttering up the feed and making it harder to find what I want to see.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some features like Stories and Reels it feels like they're slowly pushing out more basic functionality to make money rather than improving the app itself.

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's always been a mixed bag I recently had to reset my password like 5 times because I forgot again something that is literally supposed to be easy to remember. The app itself doesn't seem to have changed much but the fact that I still can't log in without messing up my account is pretty frustrating

I've been using Instagram for years and while I still enjoy it sometimes this app is getting a bit too focused on trying to keep me engaged all the time which can be annoying if I just want to post something without having to think about how it's going to look or whether it's going to get any likes.

I've been using Instagram for a few years now and while it's still my go-to platform for staying connected with friends and family I've started to feel like it's become less about sharing meaningful moments and more about curated highlight reels that are really just advertisements for products or brands I occasionally get stuck on an endless loop of watching videos from accounts I don't even remember following which can be frustrating

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if it's a good idea or not. Sometimes it feels like a great way to stay connected with friends and family but other times I just feel like I'm scrolling through a never-ending feed of stuff I don't really care about.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the creative features like Reels and IGTV I'm not sure how I feel about the new algorithm changes they made recently making it harder to discover content from accounts I actually care about

I've been using Instagram for a while now and overall I'm pretty satisfied with the app but there are some features that need work like the algorithm that changes all the time making it hard to keep up with my favorite accounts and sometimes the videos can be really slow to load which is frustrating when you're trying to relax.

I've been using Instagram for a while now and I'm still not sure if I like it. The app itself is pretty smooth and easy to navigate but sometimes the ads can be really annoying. On the other hand the features like stories and reels are super convenient. Overall its just a solid social media platform that gets the job done but doesn't really stand out from the crowd

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if it's worth the time anymore. The app is so visually driven but that's also what makes it feel shallow at times. Some of my favorite creators have started to leave or their content has changed in a way that feels really disjointed from my interests. Still can't deny that it's an easy way to get inspired by new fashion trends and music or find inspiration for creative projects

I've been using Instagram for years and while I still enjoy it some days more than others I find that my usage has become a bit stale lately they keep changing the algorithm which makes it hard to see posts from accounts I actually care about

I've been using Instagram for a while now and its features are getting more and more refined but sometimes I feel like I'm missing out on content because the algorithm is so tailored to my interests which can be both good and bad depending on how you look at it

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm not sure why people get so worked up about it. The app is fine I guess it does what it's supposed to do but sometimes the algorithm gets weird and my feed isn't very interesting anymore.

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's always been a bit hit or miss with me. Sometimes I love discovering new accounts and getting inspired by what other creators are doing but other times I feel like it's just a never-ending stream of ads and people posting stuff I don't care about. The app itself is fine and all but I wish they'd crack down on some of the more annoying things like when stories start playing automatically without letting you control it or when you try to post a pic that isn't vertical and it gets all distorted on your feed

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's still my go-to platform for staying connected with friends and family the constant algorithm changes are getting old I miss the good old days when my posts were actually seen by people who cared not now it feels like I'm just shouting into a void

I've been using Instagram for years and I still don't really get why they keep changing the layout everything was so easy to navigate back when it was just a feed no more is this change going to make the app worse?

I've been using Instagram for years and its still my go-to platform for staying connected with friends and family despite some features feeling a bit outdated like Stories and Reels which are always trying to be something new and different but sometimes it just feels like they're clashing with each other.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if I love it or hate it. Sometimes it feels like a great way to discover new content and connect with friends but other times it just seems like another social media platform where everyone's just trying to sell me something. The algorithm is also super confusing and sometimes my feed gets stuck on some obscure niche stuff that I don't even care about anymore

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some of the features like Stories and Reels I find the algorithm is so inconsistent sometimes I'll see an account that's barely active still get way more engagement than accounts that are really popular it's like there's no clear logic to it which can be frustrating when you're trying to grow your own account

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's still my favorite platform for staying connected with friends and family I have to say that recent changes are getting on my nerves they're always making updates which sometimes feel like they're working against the user experience rather than improving it like the new algorithm is supposed to be helping me discover content but honestly it feels more like it's hiding stuff from me

I was trying to get back into using Instagram after a few years and I have to say its still pretty much the same old thing. The features are still there but they've added so many more ads that it feels like you're not even on the app anymore. Still I guess if you're into that sorta thing then its okay

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some of the features like the Reels editing tool it's starting to feel like they're more focused on pushing ads than creating a user-friendly app that doesn't slow down on older devices.

I've been using Instagram for years and while its got some great features like stories and reels I'm not really sure what's changed since they added IGTV - feels like more work to create content now than ever before

I've been using Instagram for years and I gotta say it's still a solid app but sometimes it feels like they're more focused on making money than actually improving the user experience. The new algorithm change was confusing and made me unfollow a lot of accounts that I loved just because they weren't showing up in my feed anymore.

I've been using Instagram for a while now and I'm not really sure if it's still improving or just keeping up with trends but some new features are kinda cool like Reels being able to edit after posting which is pretty convenient while others feel like they're holding me back from getting more engagement on my posts.

I've been using Instagram for a while now and overall its a pretty good app I guess some features are great like the stories and reels but others are kinda meh like the feed algorithm is always changing which can be frustrating sometimes the ads can be annoying too but at least they're not as bad as Facebook's

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if it's a social media platform or just a really long photo album. The features they add are usually nice but sometimes feel like a cash grab. The algorithm is fine I guess when you're scrolling through your feed but gets all wonky when you try to find specific accounts.

I've been using Instagram for years and I still don't get why they can't just make the interface simpler it's so cluttered now and the new updates are always breaking things but at the same time I love being able to share photos with my friends and the community is really active which makes up for all the bugs

I've been using Instagram for a few years now and its still one of my favorite social media apps to keep up with friends and family who live far away. The camera is definitely getting better with each update and features like Reels are super convenient. However I wish they would clean up the clutter on my feed sometimes and make it easier to find content from accounts I actually care about.

I've been using Instagram for a while now and I'm not really sure what's changed but the new algorithm is making it harder to find content from accounts that aren't following me which isn't a huge deal but also makes the app feel more curated than it used to be

I've been using Instagram for years and while I still enjoy browsing through my favorite accounts its not the main way Im stay connected with friends and family anymore so its just one more thing to keep track of

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm neither here nor there about it I like some features like Reels are really fun to watch but the algorithm is so inconsistent sometimes my posts get tons of views on Monday but barely anything on Tuesday its all just a mystery

I've been using Instagram for years and overall I'm pretty satisfied with how things have evolved but some features like the algorithm changes are really frustrating when they impact my account's visibility.

I've tried using Instagram for a while now and I'm kinda stuck between loving it and hating it at the same time. The app's visuals are super engaging but sometimes it feels like the algorithm is more focused on showing me stuff that's just trying too hard to be relevant rather than what's actually interesting to me.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the aesthetic of the app, the algorithm is really starting to lose its effectiveness lately. Sometimes I'll post something new and not get a single engagement for days but then suddenly get flooded with comments on an old post from months ago. It's like they're trying to punish me for actually posting something worthwhile.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the features like Reels and IGTV they're getting a bit repetitive now. The algorithm still knows what I want to see even if the content is old videos of my friends or random ads. It's like they think I have no memory at all. Sometimes it feels like they're more focused on making money than improving the app.

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's still a good platform for keeping up with friends and family I'm starting to feel like it's losing its originality everything looks so curated and perfect which is just not real life

I've been using Instagram for years and its just okay I mean it's not great but it's not terrible either the interface has changed so much over time that sometimes I feel like I'm still getting used to how things work the camera features are pretty cool but sometimes the algorithm pushes posts from accounts I don't really care about and it feels like the app is more focused on trying to sell me stuff than letting me see what my friends are up to which is fine I guess

I've been using Instagram for years and while I appreciate its features like stories and reels it's become so cluttered with ads that sometimes I feel like I'm more interested in what brands are trying to sell me than what my friends are posting.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I still enjoy it sometimes it feels like they're playing games with their algorithm which makes it really hard to find content from accounts I actually care about now its like they're trying to get me to follow more creators but it's not like I'm going to start following just because they say so.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if its value to me is worth the time it takes to keep up with the ever-changing algorithm. Sometimes the app is super fun to browse through old photos of friends but other times I feel like I'm wasting my time scrolling through posts that seem like they were created just to get more likes

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the aesthetic of the app its always a struggle to find content that resonates with me sometimes you post something and 50 people like it but no one comments.
I've been using Instagram for years and while its still a solid platform I'm starting to think its value is being eaten away by TikTok's features which make IGs stories feel stale.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if it's worth the hype it gets sometimes. The app itself is fine but it feels like they're always pushing me to spend more time on it than I actually want to. Sometimes the algorithm does a good job of showing me stuff I care about but other times it just seems like it's trying to get my attention for its own sake which can be frustrating

I've been using Instagram for years and its still one of my favorite apps for staying connected with friends but the algorithm changes always make me wonder if I'm doing something wrong to get less visibility on my posts. Sometimes features are super cool like Reels or IGTV but other times it feels like they're just taking away more from what made the app good in the first place

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if it's worth the hassle. The app is easy to navigate but sometimes feels like an endless feed of things I don't really want to see. The filters are nice but the algorithm always seems to show me the same old posts from my friends who I already know what they're doing. Overall I guess Instagram is just a part of my online life like Facebook or Twitter

I was pretty meh about Instagram when I first got it but over time its grown on me a bit now Ive gotten into using the stories feature to share my daily life with friends and family and its been nice to feel more connected to them even though Im not always posting anything Im happy with the app overall
I've been using Instagram for years and I'm not sure what's changed lately but the app just feels so cluttered with ads now it's making me think of deleting it

I've been using Instagram for years and I have to say its still a pretty good app overall. The new features are nice but sometimes feel like just a bunch of bells and whistles that don't really add anything to my experience.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love catching up with friends and family it's not a substitute for human interaction. The app is still super functional and easy to use but sometimes I feel like I'm just scrolling through a highlight reel of other people's lives.
I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure what's changed recently but the app feels really different now that they removed the like button from my feed.

I've been using Instagram for a while now and overall its decent I mean it has some great features like stories and reels but sometimes the algorithm is super finicky and it shows my posts to like 5 people instead of 5000 like it should be. The new shopping feature is also pretty cool but some brands are really taking advantage of it by charging way too much for products that aren't even that special.

I've tried Instagram for a few months now and I'm still not sure if it's for me I like some of the features like stories and reels but the constant algorithm changes drive me crazy sometimes they say its to keep us engaged but honestly it feels like they're just trying to confuse us

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still trying to figure out why they keep changing the algorithm nothing feels more satisfying than scrolling through my feed only to see that all of my favorite accounts have stopped posting or are now showing me ads it's like they're sucking the life out of the app

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if it's a blessing or a curse. On one hand I love that it makes me feel more connected to my friends who are far away but on the other hand I find myself comparing my life to everyone else's curated highlight reels which is super annoying.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the creative aspect of it I'm not sure how much longer I can keep up with the constant algorithm changes that seem to favor bigger accounts over smaller ones which makes it harder to discover new artists and brands that I actually want to support It's like they're constantly trying to drive out the unique voices that make Instagram worth using in the first place

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's still one of my favorite social media platforms I'm starting to feel like it's becoming too similar to TikTok the features are so similar now that it's hard to tell them apart sometimes the new algorithm is really affecting how content appears on my feed

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still trying to figure out if its worth keeping up with. The new features are cool I guess but sometimes they feel like a hassle. I mean who needs another way to share their food pic. On the other hand the community is pretty active which is nice. It's just that sometimes it feels like everyone's just posting for likes.

I've been using Instagram for a few years now and I'm still not sure if I love it or hate it. The app is so visually appealing with all the pretty pictures but sometimes I feel like I'm just scrolling through a highlight reel of everyone else's lives without really getting to know anyone. It's also super easy to get sucked into endless feeds and lose track of time which isn't great for my mental health.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the creativity it allows users to showcase their lives and connect with others its constantly changing features sometimes make me feel like Im stuck in a never ending loop of trial and error trying new things that either dont work or are just not for me

I've been using Instagram for years and overall I'm still pretty happy with how it's evolved but at the same time I feel like it's lost that special something that made me want to check my feed every day now it just feels like a chore to sit through all the ads and curated highlight reels

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's still my go-to platform for staying connected with friends and family I have to say the algorithm changes lately feel a bit random sometimes I'll post something that gets a lot of engagement but then my content from last week is suddenly buried under 10 comments.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some of the features like Reels its always seems to be glitching out on me making me miss posts from friends and family which is frustrating because that's what makes the app worth having.

I've been using Instagram for years and while it's still my go-to platform for staying connected with friends and family the latest algorithm changes have made it feel less engaging than before I mean I used to get lost hours scrolling through feeds but now it just feels like an endless feed of ads

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some of its features like Reels and Stories I've started to feel like my feed is becoming too curated and fake everyone's highlight reels are just showcasing their best moments not their everyday life which makes me miss the good old days when we only got to see our friends' real lives.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some of its features like Reels and IGTV there's still so much clutter on my feed with ads and unwanted content that makes it feel more like a shopping list than a social network. Sometimes the app just freezes up on me too which is super frustrating

I've been using Instagram for years and I still don't really get what's changed lately feels like they're trying to make it more work than just scrolling through pics but at the same time it's good that we have features like IGTV and Reels so overall its a solid app

I've been using Instagram for years and its still one of my favorite apps the features are pretty good and the community is active but sometimes the algorithm feels a bit wonky and takes ages to show me what I'm really interested in

I've been using Instagram for years and while I still enjoy it mostly fine but some features have become really annoying lately like when they remove likes from chronological feeds or make ads more obtrusive that's just a bit too much

I was using Instagram for a few weeks and I'm still not sure if its features are worth the hassle. The new shopping feature is kind of convenient but sometimes gets in the way of my scrolling experience. On the other hand the community on here seems really supportive and it's nice to see people sharing their passions with each other. Overall I guess Instagram is okay if you're into that sort of thing

I've been using Instagram for years and it's still one of my favorite social media platforms. I love that I can post photos and videos from my phone and connect with friends and family who live far away. The app is pretty intuitive and easy to use even if you're not tech savvy like me. However I wish they would get rid of the ads on the profile pages because sometimes it looks cluttered.

I've been using Instagram for a while now and I have to say its got some okay features like the stories and reels but then again theyre all about keeping you on the app so sometimes its hard not to feel like youre missing out on whats happening in your real life

I've been using Instagram for years and while I still enjoy seeing my friends posts its not something I actively look forward to opening every day. The new features are nice but I wish they would clean up the interface a bit more some of the layout options can be really cluttered.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if I love it or hate it. On one hand it's amazing for keeping up with my favorite celebrities and influencers but on the other hand it's so time consuming scrolling through everyone else's perfectly curated feeds can be really depressing.

I've been using Instagram for a few years now and while its always been a solid platform for staying connected with friends and family its started to feel like just another social media app where people are more focused on their online persona than actual human connections.

I've been using Instagram for a few years now and I'm pretty meh about it honestly it does what it's supposed to do but there are some features that I wish they'd improve like the ability to edit captions after posting which would be really convenient

I've been using Instagram for a while now and overall its pretty meh to be honest I mean some features like stories and IGTV are really cool but others like hashtag challenges feel so forced and fake I guess its all about what type of content you want to post and who your audience is I still keep using it because I have a lot of friends on there but idk if ill ever get rid of it

I've been using Instagram for years and overall I'm satisfied with the app but sometimes I feel like the algorithm shows me more ads than actual content that my friends are posting which is kind of disappointing. The new features they added to the camera function are pretty cool and it's nice to have more options for editing photos however sometimes they crash or take a while to load which can be frustrating.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the idea of a community-driven app I've started to feel like my feed is just a curated highlight reel of others people's lives rather than something that truly reflects my own interests. The algorithm can be really unpredictable which makes it hard to discover new content I'm actually interested in.

I've been using Instagram for years and while its got some cool features like Reels I feel like it's just become too much of a clone to other apps that offer similar functionality. The aesthetic is still pretty great but sometimes I find myself scrolling through my feed feeling more bored than inspired

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm not sure what's changed but the algorithm seems to prioritize ads over my actual friends posts now its getting harder to find content that interests me without seeing a bunch of sponsored posts that feel like they're pushing something all day

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm kinda torn about it. On one hand I love that I can share my favorite moments with friends and family on the platform. But at the same time I find myself spending way too much time scrolling through feeds and comparing everyone's highlight reels which never seems to end well for me

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm decently satisfied with its features although some of them feel a bit outdated like stories disappearing after 24 hours is that really necessary. The algorithm change has also made my content reach less audience than before which kinda sucks. On the other hand new features like IGTV have been okay

I've been using Instagram for years and its still one of my go to apps for staying connected with friends and family but lately I feel like theyre trying too hard to keep me engaged which is kinda annoying sometimes

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some of the features like Stories and Reels I'm starting to feel like it's all just a bunch of noise I mean don't get me wrong the community is still super active and it's great for business owners but as a casual user I find myself getting bored with the algorithm and seeing the same posts over and over again

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some of the features like stories and IGTV it's gotten to the point where ads are taking over my feed. Sometimes I get a great photo or video but 90% of them are sponsored posts. It would be nice if you could filter out those more easily

I've been using Instagram for years and while its still one of my favorite apps to pass the time I'm starting to feel like it's getting a bit stale there are so many other social media platforms that offer similar features without all the drama and negativity

I've been using Instagram for years and I gotta say its decent platform nothing too groundbreaking about it but hey its still pretty good at what it does the UI could use a bit of an overhaul but overall its functional.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if I love it or hate it. The features are always getting updated so you never know what to expect from one day to the next. Sometimes new things come out that make me happy like Reels but then some other feature gets taken away that I actually used all the time before. Overall I guess Instagram is fine for keeping up with friends and family but it's not really my go-to app when I need something else done.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I still enjoy scrolling through my feeds sometimes it feels like the algorithm is more focused on pushing ads than showing me content from accounts I actually care about. The new feature to prioritize Reels over posts was a nice touch though!

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some of its features like the Stories and Reels updates they keep adding new stuff that doesn't really do it for me sometimes their algorithm is so confusing I mean one day my posts are getting so much engagement and the next day none at all feels like a total waste of time spent trying to get attention

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm neither here nor there about its updates - some features are nice but others feel like a chore to navigate; overall I still get more engagement on my posts than any other platform so I guess that's what matters.

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm still not sure if I love it or hate it - sometimes the features are great but other times they feel like a chore to maintain my feed feels so curated and fake compared to what's real life.

I was using Instagram for years and recently switched to TikTok and I gotta say its a very similar app just with more features like shopping and stories but sometimes the ads are super annoying and I wish they had better moderation on some of the accounts that get featured on the explore page.
I was really looking forward to trying out Instagram's new features but honestly they just feel like minor tweaks and I'm still waiting for a major overhaul of the app.

I've been using Instagram for a while now and I'm not really sure if it's worth switching to TikTok or keeping on with what I know - either app has its pros and cons which probably vary depending on who you are so I guess my experience might be pretty average

I've been using Instagram for a while now and I'm not sure if its always getting better or worse I mean some features like Reels are really great but others like the discovery page can be kinda hit or miss sometimes I also wish they would allow more free posts or something to help people discover new accounts without relying on the algorithm which is just kinda random

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm not sure what all the fuss is about some features are great others are so clunky I miss the old interface that was way more user friendly now it's just a bunch of ads and unnecessary buttons making it hard to post pics

I've been using Instagram for years and it's still pretty meh I mean it does what I need it to but it's not like it's blown my mind or anything the new features are cool I guess but they're not game changers and sometimes the app freezes on me which is super frustrating

I've been using Instagram for years and I'm not really sure what's changed lately but the app still works fine for me it's just that all my friends seem to be posting the same kinds of things over and over again which is kinda boring

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love some features like Reels and Stories it's getting harder to keep my account organized with all the ads popping up everywhere I feel like I need to pay for a premium subscription just to have an ad-free experience

I've been using Instagram for a while now and I have to say its got some great features like stories and reels that make it fun to keep up with my friends and family but sometimes the algorithm is super weird and limits what you see which can be frustrating especially when you're trying to find content from specific accounts
I've been using Instagram for years and while I love how visually appealing it is I don't really use it that much anymore because of all the ads which honestly aren't even fun anymore.

I've been using Instagram for years and while I love the creative features it's gotten so much more crowded lately which makes it harder to stand out with my own content feel like the app is trying to be too trendy all the time

I've been using Instagram for years and its a decent platform for sharing photos and videos but I'm getting really annoyed with all these ads that keep popping up on my feed and you cant even get rid of them by blocking the account or anything. It's like they're just piling it on now.

I've been using Instagram for years and while its not perfect I don't regret switching from Facebook I love that you can post stories without having to go through a bunch of steps but at the same time it's so easy to get sucked into endless scrolling which is just a waste of time
